---
title: "07 — Base Graphics"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Scatter plot

```{r fig.height=4}
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg,
  pch  = 16, # point shape (16 = filled circle)
  col  = "steelblue",
  xlab = "Weight (1000 lb)",
  ylab = "Miles per gallon",
  main = "MPG vs Weight"
)
abline(lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars), col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

## Adding to an existing plot

```{r fig.height=4}
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg, pch = 1)
points(mtcars$wt[mtcars$cyl == 8], mtcars$mpg[mtcars$cyl == 8],
  pch = 16, col = "red"
) # overlay points
lines(c(2, 5), c(30, 15), col = "blue") # add line segment
legend("topright",
  legend = c("4/6 cyl", "8 cyl"),
  pch = c(1, 16), col = c("black", "red")
)
```

## Boxplot

```{r fig.height=4}
boxplot(mpg ~ cyl,
  data = mtcars,
  xlab = "Cylinders", ylab = "MPG",
  col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow")
)
# The thick line = MEDIAN (not mean)
```

## Histogram

```{r fig.height=4}
hist(mtcars$mpg,
  breaks = 10, col = "steelblue",
  xlab = "MPG", main = "Distribution of MPG"
)
```

## Multiple plots in one window

```{r fig.height=5}
par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) # 1 row, 2 columns
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg, main = "MPG vs Weight")
plot(mtcars$hp, mtcars$mpg, main = "MPG vs HP")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) # reset
```

## Saving plots

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Open device → draw → close
pdf("plot.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg)
dev.off()

png("plot.png", width = 800, height = 600)
hist(mtcars$mpg)
dev.off()

# Copy current on-screen plot to file
dev.copy2pdf(file = "plot.pdf")
dev.copy(png, "plot.png")
dev.off()
```

## pch values (point shapes)

| pch | Shape          |
|-----|----------------|
| 1   | open circle    |
| 16  | filled circle  |
| 17  | filled triangle|
| 15  | filled square  |
| 4   | cross (×)      |
